intro: C provides total operational freedom, delegating memory safety and pointer validity entirely to the engineer's discipline. Rust implements an uncompromising, compile-time borrow checker that guarantees memory safety and data-race prevention without runtime overhead. Migrating to Rust eliminates systemic security exploits like use-after-free and buffer overflows while preserving bare-metal performance characteristics.

diffs.from: Static, weakly enforced type checking with high reliance on structural casts and void pointers.
diffs.to: Static, strongly typed system with affine types (ownership semantics) and robust algebraic data types via enums.

diffs.from: Pre-ANSI or ANSI macro-driven layout with separation of declarations (.h) and implementations (.c).
diffs.to: Modern, expression-oriented syntax with structural pattern matching, modular item scope, and integrated macro rules.

diffs.from: Explicit application-controlled manual heap layout through allocator APIs (`malloc`, `free`).
diffs.to: Compile-time ownership model enforcing strict scoping, borrowing limitations, and deterministic stack/heap cleanup (RAII).

diffs.from: Low-level OS abstractions using polling loops, `select/poll`, or external event libraries.
diffs.to: Zero-cost `async/await` state machines abstracting decoupled asynchronous runtimes like Tokio or async-std.

diffs.from: Minimal standard definitions focusing strictly on hardware I/O interfaces and platform bridges.
diffs.to: Extensive library tracking cross-platform thread synchronization, standard collections, and robust error wrappers.

examples.title: Safe Memory Allocation
examples.description: Replacing vulnerable manual heap allocations with Rust's automated RAII guarantees.

examples.title: Error Handling Optimization
examples.description: Replacing unpredictable sentinels with type-safe, compile-enforced Result variants.

tips.title: Internalize Lifetime Paradigms First
tips.body: Do not duplicate C's architecture of passing arbitrary raw pointers. Rust forces you to formally declare who owns the data and how long references exist using strict lifetime parameters (`'a`).

tips.title: Use Unsafe Code Blocks Deliberately
tips.body: When interfacing directly with hardware registers or raw hardware drivers, isolate those modules within explicit `unsafe {}` boundaries to maintain general application invariants.

tips.title: Leverage Options Instead of NULL Pointers
tips.body: Null pointers do not exist safely in Rust. Replace functions returning `NULL` with the standard `Option<T>` enum, requiring patterns to handle the `None` variation before accessing contents.

tips.title: Rely heavily on Cargo Tools
tips.body: Abandon complex Makefiles or custom build configurations. Rust utilizes Cargo to seamlessly integrate dependency resolution, builds, configurations, testing, and continuous benchmarking out-of-the-box.
